questionList.questionTitle: Employment increases during expansion
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following typically occurs during an expansionary phase of a business cycle?

optionList.optionContent: Nominal interest rates decrease.
Income taxes decrease.
The price level decreases.
Government transfer payments increase.
Employment increases.

questionOption:
A .

Nominal interest rates decrease.

B .

Income taxes decrease.

C .

The price level decreases.

D .

Government transfer payments increase.

E .

Employment increases.

questionList.questionTitle: Increased potential real GDP is economic growth
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Economic growth refers to an increase in which of the following?

optionList.optionContent: Government spending
Consumption spending
Nominal gross domestic product
Potential real gross domestic product
Household wealth

questionOption:
A .

Government spending

B .

Consumption spending

C .

Nominal gross domestic product

D .

Potential real gross domestic product

E .

Household wealth

questionList.questionTitle: Relative increases in nominal i, real i, inflation
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If both the nominal interest rate and the expected inflation rate increase, what will happen to the real interest rate?

optionList.optionContent: It will increase because the expected inflation rate has increased.
It will increase because the nominal interest rate has increased.
It will increase if the expected inflation rate increases by more than the nominal interest rate.
It will decrease because the nominal interest rate has increased.
It will decrease if the expected inflation rate increases by more than the nominal interest rate.

questionOption:
A .

It will increase because the expected inflation rate has increased.

B .

It will increase because the nominal interest rate has increased.

C .

It will increase if the expected inflation rate increases by more than the nominal interest rate.

D .

It will decrease because the nominal interest rate has increased.

E .

It will decrease if the expected inflation rate increases by more than the nominal interest rate.

questionList.questionTitle: Expansionary fiscal policy increases AD
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following statements is true about an expansionary fiscal policy?

optionList.optionContent: It decreases demand for loanable funds.
It decreases the equilibrium price level.
It decreases the equilibrium real interest rate.
It increases aggregate demand.
It increases the money supply.

questionOption:
A .

It decreases demand for loanable funds.

B .

It decreases the equilibrium price level.

C .

It decreases the equilibrium real interest rate.

D .

It increases aggregate demand.

E .

It increases the money supply.

questionList.questionTitle: Deflation increases real value of fixed incomes
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following will most likely result from deflation?

optionList.optionContent: Increased nominal interest rates
Increased business profits
Increased real value of fixed incomes
Decreased purchasing power of cash
Decreased real wealth

questionOption:
A .

Increased nominal interest rates

B .

Increased business profits

C .

Increased real value of fixed incomes

D .

Decreased purchasing power of cash

E .

Decreased real wealth

questionList.questionTitle: Fractional reserve, banks keep portion of deposits
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following is a defining characteristic of a fractional reserve banking system?

optionList.optionContent: The existence of a central bank with a monopoly on money creation
The use of paper money backed by a commodity such as gold or silver
The fact that banks retain an amount of bank reserves that is less than the amount of customer demand deposits
The requirement that banks maintain a certain percentage of their reserves as a deposit in an account at the central bank
The regulations that separate investment banking from commercial banking

questionOption:
A .

The existence of a central bank with a monopoly on money creation

B .

The use of paper money backed by a commodity such as gold or silver

C .

The fact that banks retain an amount of bank reserves that is less than the amount of customer demand deposits

D .

The requirement that banks maintain a certain percentage of their reserves as a deposit in an account at the central bank

E .

The regulations that separate investment banking from commercial banking

questionList.questionTitle: Negative AS shock increases PL, unemployment
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: A negative aggregate supply shock will result in which of the following in the short run?

optionList.optionContent: An increase in the price level and a decrease in the unemployment rate
A decrease in the price level and an increase in the unemployment rate
A decrease in both the price level and real output
An increase in both the price level and real output
An increase in both the price level and the unemployment rate

questionOption:
A .

An increase in the price level and a decrease in the unemployment rate

B .

A decrease in the price level and an increase in the unemployment rate

C .

A decrease in both the price level and real output

D .

An increase in both the price level and real output

E .

An increase in both the price level and the unemployment rate

questionList.questionTitle: Decrease taxes, adm int rates to fight recession
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following combinations of fiscal and monetary policies will correct a severe recession?

optionList.optionContent: Increasing income tax rates and increasing administered interest rates
Increasing income tax rates and decreasing administered interest rates
Decreasing income tax rates and increasing administered interest rates
Decreasing income tax rates and decreasing administered interest rates
Decreasing income tax rates and increasing the policy rate

questionOption:
A .

Increasing income tax rates and increasing administered interest rates

B .

Increasing income tax rates and decreasing administered interest rates

C .

Decreasing income tax rates and increasing administered interest rates

D .

Decreasing income tax rates and decreasing administered interest rates

E .

Decreasing income tax rates and increasing the policy rate

questionList.questionTitle: More computers from South Korea decreases US Xn
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following would decrease the United States net exports to South Korea?

optionList.optionContent: South Korean computer companies sell more computers to the United States.
South Korean insurance companies provide fewer services to the United States.
South Koreans spend more vacations in the United States.
United States banks provide more banking services to South Korea.
United States smartphone companies sell more smartphones to South Korea.

questionOption:

South Korean computer companies sell more computers to the United States.

B .

South Korean insurance companies provide fewer services to the United States.

C .

D .

E .

questionList.questionTitle: New housing increases Ig and AD
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: An increase in the purchases of newly constructed houses will result in which of the following?

optionList.optionContent: Aggregate demand will decrease as a result of a decrease in the price level.
Aggregate demand will increase as a result of an increase in investment spending.
Aggregate demand will increase as a result of an increase in exports.
Aggregate demand will not change, since consumer spending has not changed.
Aggregate demand will not change, since investment spending has not changed.

questionOption:
A .

Aggregate demand will decrease as a result of a decrease in the price level.

B .

Aggregate demand will increase as a result of an increase in investment spending.

C .

Aggregate demand will increase as a result of an increase in exports.

D .

Aggregate demand will not change, since consumer spending has not changed.

E .

Aggregate demand will not change, since investment spending has not changed.

questionList.questionTitle: 2023-Practice Exam 2-MCQ-11
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Suppose that a country's nominal gross domestic product (GDP) was $1,000 in year 1 and $2,000 in year 2. If year 1 is the base year and real GDP in year 2 was $1,000, which of the following is true?

optionList.optionContent:
A.

Prices fell by 50% between year 1 and year 2.

B.

Prices doubled between year 1 and year 2.

C.

Prices remained the same between year 1 and year 2.

D.

More goods and services were produced in year 2 than in year 1.

E.

Fewer goods and services were produced in year 2 than in year 1.

questionOption:
A.

Prices fell by 50% between year 1 and year 2.

B.

Prices doubled between year 1 and year 2.

C.

Prices remained the same between year 1 and year 2.

D.

More goods and services were produced in year 2 than in year 1.

E.

Fewer goods and services were produced in year 2 than in year 1.

B .

C .

D .

questionList.questionTitle: Rising national income, more imports, depreciation

optionList.optionContent: The demand for Country X’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.
The demand for Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will depreciate.
The demand for Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.
The supply of Country X’s currency will decrease, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.
The supply of Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country Y’s currency will depreciate.

A .

The demand for Country X’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.

B .

The demand for Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will depreciate.

C .

The demand for Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.

D .

The supply of Country X’s currency will decrease, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.

E .

The supply of Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country Y’s currency will depreciate.

Sam has a comparative advantage in mowing and an absolute advantage in raking.

C .

Sam has a comparative advantage in mowing and an absolute advantage in mowing.

D .

Bill has a comparative advantage in mowing and an absolute advantage in mowing.

E .

Bill has a comparative advantage in raking and an absolute advantage in mowing.

questionList.questionTitle: Competition doesn't cause sticky wages, prices
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: All of the following explain why prices and wages are sticky EXCEPT

optionList.optionContent: menu costs experienced by firms
efficiency wages paid to labor
misperceptions about relative prices by suppliers
competition in the business sector
labor contracts covering multiple years

questionOption:
A .

menu costs experienced by firms

B .

efficiency wages paid to labor

C .

misperceptions about relative prices by suppliers

D .

competition in the business sector

E .

labor contracts covering multiple years

questionList.questionTitle: Calculate 10% unemployment from table
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: The table above gives population and labor-market data for an economy. The unemployment rate in this economy is

optionList.optionContent: 3.3%
5%
10%
33.3%
50%

questionOption:
A .

3.3%

B .

5%

C .

10%

D .

33.3%

E .

50%

questionList.questionTitle: Deficit spending SR impact on debt, output
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: In the short run, how would a government’s budget deficit, national debt, and real output change if government spending increases with no change in taxes?

optionList.optionContent:
Deficit Debt Real Output
Increase Increase Decrease

Deficit Debt Real Output
Increase Decrease Increase

Deficit Debt Real Output
Increase Increase Increase

Deficit Debt Real Output
Decrease Decrease Increase

Deficit Debt Real Output
Decrease Increase Decrease

questionOption:
A .
Deficit Debt Real Output
Increase Increase Decrease

B .
Deficit Debt Real Output
Increase Decrease Increase

C .
Deficit Debt Real Output
Increase Increase Increase

D .
Deficit Debt Real Output
Decrease Decrease Increase

E .
Deficit Debt Real Output
Decrease Increase Decrease

questionList.questionTitle: Movement along PPC illustrates opportunty cost
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: The graph above shows the production possibilities curves (PPC) for an economy. The concept of opportunity cost is best represented by which of the following?

optionList.optionContent: A shift from PPC1 to PPC2
A movement from point A to point D
A movement from point B to point C
A movement from point C to point E
A movement from point D to point B

questionOption:
A .

A shift from PPC1 to PPC2

B .

A movement from point A to point D

C .

A movement from point B to point C

D .

A movement from point C to point E

E .

A movement from point D to point B

questionList.questionTitle: Higher interest rates cause currency appreciation
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If the central bank of Country K wishes to increase the value of its currency on foreign exchange markets, it can do which of the following?

optionList.optionContent: Buy the currencies of other countries
Increase the domestic money supply in Country K
Increase the income tax in Country K
Raise interest rates in Country K
Increase tariffs in Country K

questionOption:
A .

Buy the currencies of other countries

B .

Increase the domestic money supply in Country K

C .

Increase the income tax in Country K

D .

Raise interest rates in Country K

E .

Increase tariffs in Country K

questionList.questionTitle: Rise in expected inflation shifts SRPC right
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: An increase in the expected inflation rate will cause the

optionList.optionContent: short-run Phillips curve to shift to the left
short-run Phillips curve to shift to the right
long-run Phillips curve to shift to the left
long-run Phillips curve to shift to the right
actual inflation rate to fall below the expected inflation rate

questionOption:
A .

short-run Phillips curve to shift to the left

B .

short-run Phillips curve to shift to the right

C .

long-run Phillips curve to shift to the left

D .

long-run Phillips curve to shift to the right

E .

actual inflation rate to fall below the expected inflation rate

questionList.questionTitle: Ali's new bike purchase counts in GDP
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following is most likely included in gross domestic product?

optionList.optionContent: Matt gives his secondhand bicycle to his brother.
Sal paints his own bicycle.
Ali buys a new bicycle.
Mike buys a share of stock in a bicycle firm.
Daniel bikes to school every day.

questionOption:
A .

Matt gives his secondhand bicycle to his brother.

B .

Sal paints his own bicycle.

C .

Ali buys a new bicycle.

D .

Mike buys a share of stock in a bicycle firm.

E .

Daniel bikes to school every day.

questionList.questionTitle: Increase adm rates causes financial capital inflow
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following is likely to result in an inflow of financial capital to Country R?

optionList.optionContent: A decrease in Country R’s government budget deficit
An increase in personal income tax rates in Country R
An increase in administered interest rates by the central bank of Country R
An increase in Country R’s trade surplus
Increased political instability in Country R

questionOption:
A .

A decrease in Country R’s government budget deficit

B .

An increase in personal income tax rates in Country R

C .

An increase in administered interest rates by the central bank of Country R

D .

An increase in Country R’s trade surplus

E .

Increased political instability in Country R

questionList.questionTitle: Calculate maximum new loans at $110 million
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: A bank has $200 million in demand deposits and $150 million in reserves. The reserve ratio is 20 percent. What is the maximum amount of loans the bank can make from its reserves?

optionList.optionContent: $750 million
$150 million
$110 million
$50 million
$40 million

questionOption:
A .

$750 million

B .

$150 million

C .

$110 million

D .

$50 million

E .

$40 million

questionList.questionTitle: AD shift does not shift long run Phillips curve
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following is true about the Phillips curve?

optionList.optionContent: A change in aggregate demand does not shift the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC).
A change in aggregate demand does not cause a movement along the short-run Phillips curve (SRPC).
The LRPC shows the trade-off between unemployment and inflation but the SRPC does not.
Changes in expected inflation affect the LRPC only.
Negative supply shocks affect the LRPC only.

questionOption:
A .

A change in aggregate demand does not shift the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC).

B .

A change in aggregate demand does not cause a movement along the short-run Phillips curve (SRPC).

C .

The LRPC shows the trade-off between unemployment and inflation but the SRPC does not.

D .

Changes in expected inflation affect the LRPC only.

E .

Negative supply shocks affect the LRPC only.

questionList.questionTitle: Balanced budget keeps national debt constant
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: When the total amount the government spends equals tax revenues in any given year, which of the following must remain constant?

optionList.optionContent: The real interest rate
The national debt
Real gross domestic product
The price level
The money supply

questionOption:
A .

The real interest rate

B .

The national debt

C .

Real gross domestic product

D .

The price level

E .

The money supply

questionList.questionTitle: Above NRU, nominal wages rise, SRAS shifts left
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: How does the automatic adjustment mechanism move the economy to potential real gross domestic product (GDP) in the long run when current real GDP is above potential GDP?

optionList.optionContent: Nominal wages fall, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the right.
Nominal wages fall, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the left.
Nominal wages do not change, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the right.
Nominal wages rise, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the right.
Nominal wages rise, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the left.

questionOption:
A .

Nominal wages fall, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the right.

B .

Nominal wages fall, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the left.

C .

Nominal wages do not change, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the right.

D .

Nominal wages rise, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the right.

E .

Nominal wages rise, shifting the short-run aggregate supply curve to the left.

questionList.questionTitle: Nominal interest rates include expected inflation
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following statements about inflation is true?

optionList.optionContent: The expected inflation rate is the difference between nominal and real interest rates.
Low expected inflation rates lead to high inflation rates.
Lenders lose from expected inflation.
Lenders gain from unexpected inflation.
Workers lose from expected inflation.

questionOption:
A .

The expected inflation rate is the difference between nominal and real interest rates.

B .

Low expected inflation rates lead to high inflation rates.

C .

Lenders lose from expected inflation.

D .

Lenders gain from unexpected inflation.

E .

Workers lose from expected inflation.

questionList.questionTitle: Opportunity cost is value of next best option
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: The opportunity cost of an activity is

optionList.optionContent: the amount of time spent on the activity
the value of the benefit received from performing the activity
the value of the forgone benefit of the next best alternative
zero if the activity offered no benefits
the sum of benefits from all the sacrificed alternatives

questionOption:
A .

the amount of time spent on the activity

B .

the value of the benefit received from performing the activity

C .

the value of the forgone benefit of the next best alternative

D .

zero if the activity offered no benefits

E .

the sum of benefits from all the sacrificed alternatives

questionList.questionTitle: Spending on schools, infrastructure creates growth
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Increased spending on which of the following contributes most to long-term economic growth?

optionList.optionContent: Social security and other transfer payments
New automobiles and homes
Education and infrastructure
Imported consumer goods
Interest payments on national debt

questionOption:
A .

Social security and other transfer payments

B .

New automobiles and homes

C .

Education and infrastructure

D .

Imported consumer goods

E .

Interest payments on national debt

questionList.questionTitle: More imports causes more financial capital inflow
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If consumers in Canada increase their demand for products that are manufactured in India, which of the following will occur?

optionList.optionContent: The Indian rupee will depreciate.
The Canadian dollar will appreciate.
India’s financial capital inflow will increase.
Canada’s financial capital inflow will increase.
The supply of Canadian dollars will decrease.

questionOption:
A .

The Indian rupee will depreciate.

B .

The Canadian dollar will appreciate.

C .

India’s financial capital inflow will increase.

D .

Canada’s financial capital inflow will increase.

E .

The supply of Canadian dollars will decrease.

questionList.questionTitle: Recession - monetary policy, business confidence

subjectiveQuestionList.questionContent:
Include correctly labeled diagrams, if useful or required, in explaining your answers. A correctly labeled diagram must have all axes and curves clearly labeled and must show directional changes. If the question prompts you to “Calculate,” you must show how you arrived at your final answer.

 

Assume a country’s economy is currently in recession.

(a) Draw a correctly labeled graph of the long-run aggregate supply, short-run aggregate supply, and aggregate demand curves, and show each of the following.

  • (i) Current real output, labeled Y1, and current price level, labeled PL1

    (ii) Full employment output, labeled Yf

(b) Assuming the banking system has ample reserves, identify one action the central bank can take to help the economy recover from the recession.

(c) Draw a correctly labeled graph of the reserve market, and show the impact of the central bank’s action identified in part (b) on the policy rate.

(d) On your graph for part (a), show the effect of the central bank’s action identified in part (b) on real output and the price level.

(e) Assume there is an increase in business confidence as a result of the central bank’s action.

  • (i) What will happen to the demand for capital goods?

    (ii) Draw a correctly labeled graph of the loanable funds market, and show the effect of the change identified in part (e)(i) on the real interest rate.

(f) Given your answer to part (e), what is the effect on potential real output in the long run? Explain.

Respond to all parts of the question:

questionList.questionTitle: 2023-Practice Exam 2-FRQ-2

subjectiveQuestionList.questionContent:
The exchange rate between the Australian dollar and the Indian rupee is determined in a flexible foreign exchange market.

(a) Assume India is currently in recession. What fiscal policy action could the Indian government take to eliminate the recession?

(b) What would be the effect of the fiscal policy action identified in part (a) on interest rates in India?

(c) Draw a correctly labeled graph of the foreign exchange market for the Australian dollar. Show on your graph the impact of the change in interest rates identified in part (b) on each of the following.

    1. The supply of Australian dollars
    2. The equilibrium exchange rate of the Australian dollar

(d) What would be the effect of the change in the exchange rate identified in part (c)(ii) on Australian exports?

(e) What would be the effect of the change in Australian exports identified in part (d) on Australian unemployment?

questionList.questionTitle: 2016 Macro data for Pattiland - GDP, unemployment

subjectiveQuestionList.questionContent:
Include correctly labeled diagrams, if useful or required, in explaining your answers. A correctly labeled diagram must have all axes and curves clearly labeled and must show directional changes. If the question prompts you to “Calculate,” you must show how you arrived at your final answer.

 

Gross domestic product (GDP), unemployment, inflation, and economic growth are key measures of a country’s economic performance.

The following statistics are from the country of Pattiland in 2016; dollar values are measured in 2016 dollars.

(a) Calculate each of the following and show your work.

  • (i) Nominal GDP

    (ii) Real GDP

    (iii) Real GDP per capita

(b) Debbie is a citizen of the country of Wayland and owns a computer software firm in Pattiland. Is the output produced by Debbie’s computer software firm included in Pattiland’s nominal GDP calculated in part (a)? Explain.

(c) The GDP deflator for Pattiland in 2015 was 110. In 2016 was Pattiland experiencing inflation or deflation, or is there insufficient information to determine Pattiland’s economic condition? Explain.

(d) Calculate the unemployment rate in Pattiland. Show your work.

Respond to all parts of the question.

template.templateName: 模考模板- AP宏观经济

partList.directionsText:
MACROECONOMICS
SECTION Ⅰ
Time - 1 hour and 10 minutes
60 Questions


Directions: Each of the questions or incomplete statements below is followed by five suggested answers or completions. Select the one that is best in each case and then fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet.

MACROECONOMICS
SECTION Ⅱ
Total Time - 1 hour
Reading Period - 10 minutes
Writing Period - 50 minutes
3 Questions


Directions: You are advised to spend the first 10 minutes reading all of the questions and planning your answers. You will then have 50 minutes to answer all three of the following questions. You may begin writing your responses before the reading period is over. lt is suggested that you spend approximately half your time on the first question and divide the remaining time equally between the next two questions. Include correctly labeled diagrams, if useful or required, in explaining your answers. A correctly labeled diagram must have all axes and curves clearly labeled and must show directional changes. lf the question prompts you to "Calculate," you must show how you arrived at your final answer. Use a pen with black or dark blue ink.

You may plan your answers in this orange booklet, but no credit will be given for anything written in this booklet. You will only earn credit for what you write in the separate Free Response booklet.
